reasons: Minsitthar directly limits Harith's repeated dash pattern.
Harith loses major fight value when he cannot freely Chrono Dash inside Zaman Force.

proof.summary: Harith wants repeated Chrono Dash uptime, while Minsitthar's King’s Calling restricts movement skills inside the field.
worksBestWhen: Minsitthar casts King’s Calling after Harith commits.
Teammates focus Harith while his dash pattern is restricted.

reasons: Khufra punishes Harith's blink and dash movement with Bouncing Ball.
Khufra can stand between Harith and the backline, forcing Harith to delay or misroute his dash chain.

proof.summary: Harith needs repeated dash access, while Khufra's Bouncing Ball punishes blink or dash attempts around him.
worksBestWhen: Khufra holds Bouncing Ball for Harith's commit.
Khufra protects the backline instead of chasing too far.
failureCases: Khufra uses anti-dash before Harith enters.

reasons: Phoveus punishes Harith's repeated Chrono Dash usage because Harith needs frequent dashes to maintain pressure, shield uptime, and repositioning inside fights.
Harith becomes less free to chain dashes aggressively when each dash commitment can create a punish window for Phoveus.

proof.summary: Harith repeatedly uses Chrono Dash, while Phoveus benefits when enemies blink, dash, or are displaced near him, enabling repeated punishment.
worksBestWhen: Phoveus stays close enough to Harith's fight zone.
Harith commits into Zaman Force near Phoveus or Phoveus's team.
failureCases: Harith plays outside Phoveus's trigger range.
Phoveus cannot mark or reach Harith before Harith resets.
Harith's team kites Phoveus before he converts the anti-dash value.

reasons: Obsidia can prevent Harith from using displacement skills during her tether window, directly cutting off the dash chain Harith needs to keep fighting.
If Obsidia lands Hunt of Bone after Harith commits, Harith can be forced to stay in a punishable position instead of resetting his position with Chrono Dash.

proof.summary: Harith wants to keep dashing with Chrono Dash, while Obsidia can tether him and prevent displacement skills during the effect.
worksBestWhen: Obsidia lands Hunt of Bone after Harith commits.
Obsidia has enough damage or follow-up to punish Harith during the tether.
failureCases: Harith baits the ultimate before committing Zaman Force.
Harith's team peels Obsidia during the tether window.

reasons: Kaja can suppress Harith during his commit window and stop his dash cycle long enough for allied burst.
Kaja is reliable if he gets in range, but he needs follow-up damage and good timing.

proof.summary: Harith wants continuous dash uptime, while Kaja can suppress and drag him out of his preferred fight pattern.
worksBestWhen: Kaja waits until Harith commits into range.
Allies are ready to burst during suppression.
failureCases: Harith baits Kaja's ultimate with shield and mobility.
Kaja catches Harith without enough follow-up damage.
